records.name: Persistent state: The sandbox preserves variables, imported modules, and file state across multiple tool calls within the same agent session
Targeted Isolation: Executes agent code in a process-level isolated sandbox environment for security.
Flexible compute configurations: Sandboxes can be configured with specific programming languages (Python, JavaScript), CPU, and memory sizes.
Sandbox clean up: Sandboxes can be explicitly deleted by the agent or automatically expire via a configurable TTL.
Code with data analysis: Supports uploading data files up to 100 MB and running multiple code-based analyses without reloading data between runs.

records.name: PlaywrightComputer: A concrete implementation of the BaseComputer interface that drives a Chromium browser via Playwright for agent-controlled browsing.
Permission modes
records.scope_note: Global permission modes (default, acceptEdits, plan, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions) control how Claude uses tools across an entire session, ranging from fully restricted to auto-approved.

lifecycle.note: Auto-triage → confirmed (grounding 1.00, classification 1.00). Permission modes (default, acceptEdits, plan, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions) are a specific sub-feature of Claude Code's guardrails & safety system. They govern how broadly or restrictively Claude is allowed to use

records.name: plan mode lets Claude explore and propose changes without auto-approving file edits
auto mode uses a model classifier to automatically approve or deny each tool call with background safety checks
Auto mode (classifier-driven) automatically allows safe edits/commands and blocks or surfaces destructive ones
Dynamic permission mode switching mid-session during streaming
ExitPlanMode requires the agent to present a plan for human approval before exiting plan mode

records.scope_note: Operators define declarative Allow, Ask, and Deny rule lists (allowedTools / disallowedTools) that pre-approve, prompt for, or block specific tools and tool calls.

records.name: Declarative settings.json permission rules as a project-level configuration source
Permission rule syntax uses Tool(specifier) supporting exact matches, glob wildcards, and domain scoping
Tool-specific permission rules allow or deny tools by name
Tiered permission system (read-only, bash commands, file modification)
Read-only commands (ls, cat, grep, find) run without a permission prompt
Compound command safety requires each subcommand to independently satisfy permission rules
Subagent tool lists restrict spawned subagents to a specified set of tools
Permission Required gating on certain tools before invocation
canUseTool callback
records.scope_note: A runtime callback pauses agent execution and surfaces tool-use approval requests to users to interactively approve or deny individual tool calls not resolved by rules.

lifecycle.note: Auto-triage → confirmed (grounding 1.00, classification 1.00). The canUseTool callback is a developer-facing runtime hook that pauses agent execution to surface tool-use approval requests to users, enabling interactive approve/deny decisions for individual tool calls not resolved

records.name: Tool approval lets users allow or deny individual tool calls, with Claude receiving a denial message
Approve with changes modifies tool inputs before execution to sanitize parameters or scope access
Approve and remember persists approved rules to local settings to skip future prompts
AskUserQuestion tool interrupts execution to ask structured multiple-choice clarifying questions
Defer hook decision exits the process and resumes later from a persisted session
